# IL1B — Interleukin 1 Beta

## Personal Genotype

| SNP | Genotype | Significance | Tier |
|-----|----------|-------------|------|
| rs16944 | G;G | Increased IL-1β expression, higher inflammatory baseline | E2 |

## Health Relevance

IL1B encodes the pro-inflammatory cytokine interleukin-1 beta. The rs16944 G;G genotype is associated with:

- **Higher baseline inflammation** — CRP levels tend to run higher (E2)
- **SSRI non-remission risk** — OR 1.74 in single study (E3-E4, unreplicated)
- **IBS symptom modulation** — inflammatory component of IBS-D (E3)

## Drug Interactions

- May affect SSRI response via neuroinflammation pathway
- Anti-inflammatory strategies (omega-3, curcumin) may be supportive

## Gene-Gene Interactions

- [[IL6]] — co-regulation of inflammatory cascade
- [[CYP2D6]] — sertraline metabolism affects downstream inflammatory response

## What Changes This

- Regular exercise reduces IL-1β expression (E2)
- Omega-3 supplementation (EPA > DHA) has modest effect (E2)
- Sleep quality strongly modulates inflammatory markers (E2)
- Stress management reduces acute IL-1β spikes (E3)

## Action Items

- [ ] Incorporate SSRI non-remission finding from research [priority:: medium] [context:: vault-maintenance] [due:: 2026-04-10]
